title: Love Island
themes: British vs. American versions as their own debate

characters.name: Iain Stirling
characters.archetype: The narrator (UK version). Scottish wit over every dramatic moment. The best part of the UK version.

characters.name: The Islanders
characters.archetype: The contestants — typically 10-12 to start, paired in couples. Couples shift over the season.

characters.name: Casa Amor cohort
characters.archetype: The 'bombshells' brought in mid-season to test couples. Often ends marriages.

characters.name: Maya Jama
characters.archetype: Current UK host. Replaced Laura Whitmore. Beloved.

characters.name: Caroline Flack
characters.archetype: Original UK host (2015-2020). Tragically died by suicide in 2020 — show added mental health protocols after.

characters.name: Sarah Hyland
characters.archetype: Current US host. Replaced Arielle Vandenberg.

signature_quotes: It's giving 'I have something to say.'
viral_moments: Casa Amor recouplings — when most relationships shatter.
The first 'I got a text!' moment of the season.
Movie night challenges — when villa drama gets edited together.
The Heart Rate challenge dance moments.
'Tommy from Bury' Tommy Fury winning S5 with Molly-Mae and becoming a real-world Manchester power couple.
memes: 'I got a text!' as the universal 'wait, important news!' meme.
'My head's been turned' for any minor reconsideration of anything.
'It's a recoupling' applied to NFL trade deadlines, NBA free agency, fantasy drafts.
Iain Stirling's narration applied to every mundane situation on the internet.
Casa Amor reveals as the most-anticipated TV moment of the year for British audiences.
'Loyalty' as a meme — characters who prove they had none.
off_screen_drama: Multiple Islander deaths post-show triggered industry-wide mental health reforms (UK).
Caroline Flack's tragic death (former host) led to industry-wide changes around press coverage.
Mike Thalassitis (S3) and Sophie Gradon (S2) both died after appearing on the show.
The US version (Peacock) has its own fanbase but the UK is the gold standard.
Tommy Fury and Molly-Mae Hague became the show's first real-life couple to have a child (Bambi, 2023). Then split in 2024.
'Maura, Curtis, Amy' love triangle in S5 became foundational reality TV viewing.
Ekin-Su (S8 winner) became one of the UK's biggest reality TV stars.
running_jokes: Every season has the 'no one is here for the right reasons' meme.
The 'unbothered' Islander who gets the most camera time.
Movie nights edited to expose every contradiction — pure chaos TV.
The 'they're best friends now' postscript that lasts 6 weeks.
Producers timing bombshells perfectly for max drama.
Iain Stirling making the smallest moment hilarious.
The 'this couple is genuine' couples breaking up by Christmas.
